info.title: Release Management API
info.description: **Looking for the old API?** You can find it here: V1 API spec The old API will be deprecated on February 1st 2027. Make sure that you have migrated to the new API before then. View the V1 → V2 Migration Guide

get.description: Gives back a paginated list of apps your user has access to for the workspace specified based on given filters.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Specifies which Bitrise Project you want to get associated apps for

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter for a specific mobile platform between your apps

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by bundle ID (for iOS), package name (for Android), or app title. (case sensitive)

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of apps returned per request

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set in a paginated scenario

post.description: Creates an app for the workspace.

201.description: The created app with default framework

value.message: The given platform is invalid. Valid platforms are: "ios", "android"
Validation failed: Framework is not included in the list

framework: is not included in the list

value.message: param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: platform
param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: store_app_name
You need an active store connection for connecting your app.

id.description: An uuidV4 identifier for your new app

manual_connection.description: If set to true it indicates a manual connection (bypassing using store api keys) and requires giving "store_app_name" as well. This can be especially useful for enterprise apps.

platform.description: The mobile platform of your app

store_app_id.description:
The store identifier for your app. 
- In case of iOS platform it is the bundle id. Check the [property description](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/information_property_list/cfbundleidentifier).
- In case of Android platform it is the package name. Check the [documentation](https://developer.android.com/build/configure-app-module#set_the_application_id).

store_app_name.description: If you have no active app store API keys added on Bitrise, you can decide to add your app manually by giving the app's name as well while indicating manual connection with the similarly named boolean flag.

store_credential_id.description:
If you have credentials added on Bitrise, you can decide to select one for your app. 
- In case of iOS platform it will be an Apple API credential id.
- In case of Android platform it will be a Google Service credential id.

framework.description: The framework used to build the app. Defaults to 'other' if not provided.

post.summary: Creates an App Store review version
post.description: Creates an App Store version with the specified version_string, which can be used to submit for App Store review. - iOS only

value.message: This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

app_id.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app.

get.summary: Gets the App Store draft version
get.description: The draft version is the latest unreleased version—the one that can be submitted for App Store review. If no such version exists, a draft version can be created. - IOS only

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app.

value.message: Draft version not found on App Store.
This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

patch.summary: Updates the App Store draft version
patch.description: Updates the current App Store draft version—the latest unreleased version that can be submitted for App Store review. A local app version with the target version_string must already exist. - iOS only

value.message: Could not find related app_version: version-without-a-release

409.description: Draft version is not in an editable state

value.message: The App Store version's status is not in an editable state.
value.details: Either complete the process in App Store Connect or cancel any outstanding reviews so that Release Management can continue the release flow here: https://appstoreconnect.apple.com/apps/1234/distribution/ios/version/inflight.

value.message: This benefit requires a Standard license for the connected app.

app_id.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app.

get.summary: Gets the list of the installable artifacts.
get.description: Gives back a list of installable artifacts.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app the artifact is connected to.

parameters.name: artifact_type
parameters.description: Filter for a specific artifact type/extension.

parameters.name: after_date
parameters.description: A date in ISO 8601 string format specifying the start of the interval when the installable artifact created/uploaded. This value will be defaulted to 1 month ago if `distribution_ready` filter is not set or set to `false`.

parameters.name: before_date
parameters.description: A date in ISO 8601 string format specifying the end of the interval when the installable artifact created/uploaded. This value will be defaulted to the current time if `distribution_ready` filter is not set or set to `false`.

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description: Filter for the Bitrise CI branch the artifact has been generated on. (if applicable)

parameters.name: distribution_ready
parameters.description: Filter for distribution ready installable artifacts. This means `.apk` and `.ipa` (with distribution type `ad-hoc`, `development`, or `enterprise`) installable artifacts.

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of installable artifacts returned per request.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set in a paginated scenario.

parameters.name: platform
parameters.description: Filter for a specific mobile platform between your installable artifacts.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by version, filename, or build number (Bitrise CI). (case sensitive)

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Filter for the source of the installable artifacts.

parameters.name: store_signed
parameters.description: Filter for store ready installable artifacts. This means signed `.aab` and `.ipa` (with distribution type `app-store`) installable artifacts.

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Filter for the version this artifact created for.This field is required if the `distribution_ready` filter is set to `true`.

parameters.name: workflow
parameters.description: Filter for the Bitrise CI workflow this artifact has been generated by. (if applicable)

value.message: param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: app_id

delete.description: Deletes an API-uploaded installable artifact of an app.

422.description: Installable artifact has been uploaded through CI

value.message: Only artifacts uploaded using the Release Management Public API can be deletedthrough this endpoint.

get.description: Returns the details of a single installable artifact.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the installable artifact.

value.message: The installable artifact cannot be found.

patch.summary: Changes whether public install page should be available for the installable artifact or not.
patch.description: Changes whether public install page should be available for the installable artifact or not.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier for the installable artifact.

200.description: Enables public install page for installable artifact

value.message: The installable artifact cannot be found.
Can't enable public install page because no install methods are allowed.

with_public_page.description: Boolean flag for enabling/disabling public install page for the installable artifact.

get.summary: Gets the status and reason of an installable artifact.
get.description:
Gets the processing and upload status of an installable artifact. Status can be one of:
- 'upload_requested': This status is given back when an upload has been requested for the installable artifact, but didn't get uploaded yet.
- 'uploaded': This status indicates that the installable artifact has been uploaded, but not yet processed.
- 'processed_valid': This status means that the uploaded installable artifact has been successfully processed and that it is a valid artifact to be used for either build distribution or store release.
- 'processed_invalid': This means that the uploaded installable artifact has been processed and that it is an invalid artifact, it cannot be used neither for build distribution nor for store release.
- 'ready_to_upload': This status is only given to installable artifacts that are created using the deploy step on Bitrise CI. For API uploaded artifacts this will never be set.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier for the installable artifact.

200.description: Gets installable artifact status with reason

get.summary: Generates an upload url for an installable artifact.
get.description: Generates a signed upload url valid for 1 hour for an installable artifact to be uploaded to Bitrise.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: An uuidV4 identifier for the installable artifact.

parameters.name: app_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the app the artifact is connected to.

parameters.name: file_name
parameters.description: The name of the installable artifact file (with extension) to be uploaded to Bitrise.

parameters.name: file_size_bytes
parameters.description: The size of the file in bytes.

parameters.name: with_public_page
parameters.description: Optionally, you can enable public install page for your artifact. This can only be enabled by Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins. Changing this value without proper permissions will result in an error. The default value is false.

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description: Optionally you can add the CI branch the artifact has been generated on.

parameters.name: workflow
parameters.description: Optionally you can add the CI workflow this artifact has been generated by.

value.message: param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: app_id
param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: file_name
param is missing or the value is empty or invalid: file_size_bytes
The platform of the artifact does not match the platform of the given connected app.
You have no permissions to enable public install page. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can change this value.

get.description: Gets what to test information for an installable artifact.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier for the installable artifact.

200.description: What to test does not exist

patch.description: Update what to test information for an installable artifact.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier for the installable artifact.

204.description: What to test exists and sending empty what to test

what_to_test.description: The what to test information for the installable artifact. This should be a string with a maximum length of 4000 characters. To remove the what to test, send an empty string.

get.summary: Lists custom access links for an installable artifact
get.description: Returns a paginated list of custom access links for the given installable artifact.

parameters.name: installable_artifact_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the installable artifact.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specifies which page should be returned from the whole result set.

parameters.name: items_per_page
parameters.description: Specifies the maximum number of items returned per request.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: The field to sort results by. Defaults to name.

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: The sort direction. Defaults to asc.

value.message: The installable artifact cannot be found.
You have no permissions to list custom access links. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can list custom access links.

post.summary: Creates a custom access link for an installable artifact
post.description: Creates a shareable custom access link for the given installable artifact.

parameters.name: installable_artifact_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the installable artifact.

201.description: Creates link and notifies an external tester group

details.expires_at: Expiry date must be a valid ISO8601 date-time.

details.access_code: Access code must be between 8 and 64 characters.

value.message: Only external tester groups can be notified via an access link
The provided artifact cannot be used for tester group notifications, id: `5738cc8d-8b88-4791-b023-13f7023def3f`
The installable artifact cannot be found.
You have no permissions to create a custom access link. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can create custom access links.

name.description: A human-readable label for the access link. Defaults to the tester group name when tester_group_id is provided.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime string after which the link will no longer be accessible. Omit for a non-expiring link.

access_code.description: Optional access code (8–64 characters) required to unlock the install page.

tester_group_id.description: Optional ID of an external tester group to notify with this link after creation.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a custom access link. The install page URL will no longer be accessible after deletion.

parameters.name: installable_artifact_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the installable artifact.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the custom access link.

value.message: The installable artifact cannot be found.
You have no permissions to delete a custom access link. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can delete custom access links.

patch.description: Updates the name, expiry, or access code of an existing custom access link.

parameters.name: installable_artifact_id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the installable artifact.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier of the custom access link.

details.access_code: Access code must be between 8 and 64 characters.

details.expires_at: Expiry date must be a valid ISO8601 date-time.

value.message: The installable artifact cannot be found.
You have no permissions to update a custom access link. Only Admins, Owners and Workspace Admins can update custom access links.

name.description: A human-readable label for the access link. Existing value is preserved when omitted.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime string after which the link will no longer be accessible. Pass null to remove expiry.

access_code.description: Optional access code (8–64 characters) required to unlock the install page. Pass null to remove the access code.

teams_webhook_url.description: You can get notifications about release updates on Teams by setting this value. Make sure to send the properly formatted webhook url. Regex for validation: ^https:\/\/(?:.*\.webhook|outlook)\.office(?:365)?\.com\/webhookb2\/.*

automation.description: Automation events (workflow or pipeline runs) setup for specific event types. Sending this value will replace the possibly already existing automation list.

events.description: A list of event names that will trigger this webhook. These events correspond to specific actions or states in the release process.

all_events.description: Indicates whether this webhook should be triggered for all events. Set to true to enable the webhook for every event.

webhook_configuration_id.description: The unique identifier of the webhook, that can be found in the workspace integration settings. This ID links the webhook to the workspace.

outgoing_webhooks.description: COMING SOON. A collection of outgoing webhooks configured to trigger for specific event types. Each webhook can be associated with one or more events or set to trigger for all events.

get.summary: Gets the status and reason of a public asset.
get.description:
Gets the processing and upload status of a public asset. Status can be one of:
- 'upload_requested': This status is given back when an upload has been requested for the public asset, but didn't get uploaded yet.
- 'uploaded': This status indicates that the public asset has been uploaded, but not yet processed.
- 'processed_valid': This status means that the uploaded public asset has been successfully processed and that it is a valid artifact to be used for either build distribution or store release.
- 'processed_invalid': This means that the uploaded public asset has been processed and that it is an invalid artifact, it cannot be used neither for build distribution nor for store release.
- 'ready_to_upload': This status is only given to public assets that are created using the deploy step on Bitrise CI. For API uploaded artifacts this will never be set.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier for the public asset.

200.description: Gets public asset status with reason

value.status_reason: The asset has been processed and ready to be used.

get.summary: Generates a signed upload url valid for 1 hour for a public asset to be uploaded to Bitrise.
get.description:
NOTE:

parameters.name: file_name

parameters.name: file_size_bytes
parameters.description: The size of the file in bytes.

delete.description: Deletes an app - only workspace owner can do this, needs standard license for the app.

get.description: Gives back an app your user has access to for the workspace.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The uuidV4 identifier for your app.

404.description: Store app id cannot be found in Store

value.message: Package name cannot be found in Google Play Console.

connect_to_store.description:
If true, will check app validity against the Apple App Store or Google Play Store (dependent on the platform of your app).
This means, that the already set or just given store_app_id will be validated against the Store, using the already set or just given store credential id.

store_app_id.description:
The store identifier for your app. You can change the previously set store_app_id to match the one in the App Store or Google Play depending on the app platform. 
This is especially useful if you want to connect your app with the store as the system willvalidate the given store_app_id against the Store.
- In case of iOS platform it is the bundle id. Check the [property description](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/information_property_list/cfbundleidentifier).
- In case of Android platform it is the package name. Check the [documentation](https://developer.android.com/build/configure-app-module#set_the_application_id).

store_credential_id.description:
If you have credentials added on Bitrise, you can decide to select one for your app. 
- In case of iOS platform it will be an Apple API credential id.
- In case of Android platform it will be a Google Service credential id.

baseURL.description: The server base URL with the scheme.

token.description: PAT necessary to use API calls
token.name: authorization
